task:
Update the wayfinder map #105 on shivam2014/pi-files to reflect the new simplified loop direction. Also update/close old tickets that no longer apply.

**Step 1: Update map #105 body**

Write the new body to /tmp/updated-map.md using the write tool, then use gh to update:

```bash
gh issue edit 105 --repo shivam2014/pi-files --body-file /tmp/updated-map.md
```

New body content for /tmp/updated-map.md:

```markdown
## Destination

Add loop mode to the orchestrator extension — the orchestrator keeps delegating with structured feedback cycles until a well-defined success condition is met. No new LoopController or LoopState types. The orchestrator IS the loop: it delegates, evaluates results against user-provided criteria, re-delegates with feedback, and repeats until done or hard-stopped.

## Notes

- Orchestrator extension: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/orchestrator/ (working copy), ~/pi-files/extensions/orchestrator/ (git repo)
- Flat TypeScript module tree. Key modules: delegate-pipeline.ts, delegate-controller.ts, plan-tool.ts, plan-panel.ts, activity-feed.ts
- Pipeline: delegate-tool.ts → DelegateController.validate() → ScopeManager.writeScope() → SubagentRunner → tool guard → output → plan step finalized
- The orchestrator already delegates, receives results, advances plan steps. Loop = orchestrator not stopping after one plan.
- Prior pi loops: pi-ralph-loop (completion markers), pi-autoresearch (confidence scoring), @pi-agents/loop (scheduler)
- Claude Code /goal: evaluator model checks completion after each turn
- The loop must have: well-defined goal, measurable success criteria, who evaluates, who implements, hard stops

## Decisions so far

- [Loop Step Schema Design](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/106) — ✅ Resolved: initial schema designed (5 new types). **Superseded** — new direction is simpler, no new types needed. Orchestrator is the loop.
- [Completion Detection Mechanism](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/107) — Renamed to "Evaluation Mechanism" (new direction)
- [LoopController Integration](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/110) — **Superseded** — no LoopController. Orchestrator handles loop natively.
- [Plan Panel UI for Loops](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/109) — Still relevant (how loop progress shows in widget)
- [Activity Feed Loop Events](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/111) — Still relevant (iteration events in feed)
- [Token Budget Controls](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/108) — Still relevant (hard stopping conditions)

## Not yet specified

- How orchestrator gathers loop setup info from user (interactive prompts vs structured input)
- Evaluation mechanism (reviewer delegation vs self-evaluation vs both)
- Feedback format (what reviewer sends back to orchestrator for coder)
- How orchestrator re-plans after each iteration
- How loop interacts with existing plan() system (replace? wrap? separate command?)
- Persistence of loop state across restarts

## Out of scope

- **Time-based /loop (scheduler)** — defer to @pi-agents/loop or @presmihaylov/pi-loop
- **New agent loop** — delegate() already is one
- **Full pi-ralph-loop scaffolding** — different approach
- **LoopController, LoopState, LoopIterationRecord** — superseded by simpler design
- **Evaluator model selection UI** — Phase 4 if demand exists
- **Cross-loop state sharing** — no use case yet

## Blocking Edges

**Frontier** (unblocked, takeable now):
- [Loop Setup UX](#) — How orchestrator gathers goal, success criteria, evaluator, max iterations
- [Evaluation Mechanism](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/107) — How results are scored against rubric
- [Token Budget Controls](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/108) — Hard stopping conditions

**Blocked** (waiting on frontier):
- [Plan Panel UI](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/109) — blocked by Loop Setup UX
- [Activity Feed Events](https://github.com/shivam2014/pi-files/issues/111) — blocked by Loop Setup UX
- [Feedback Loop Mechanics](#) — blocked by Evaluation Mechanism
```

**Step 2: Close superseded tickets**

```bash
gh issue close 110 --repo shivam2014/pi-files --comment "Superseded: new direction is simpler — orchestrator IS the loop, no LoopController needed. See updated map #105."
```

**Step 3: Rename ticket #107**

```bash
gh issue edit 107 --repo shivam2014/pi-files --title "Evaluation Mechanism" --body 'Parent: #105

## Question

How does the orchestrator evaluate results against success criteria in a loop?

The user sets up a loop with a scoring rubric (e.g., "reviewer must score ≥9 on all aspects"). After each delegation, the orchestrator sends output to an evaluator (reviewer specialist) who scores it. If score passes → done. If not → orchestrator re-delegates with feedback.

Decide:
- Who evaluates? Reviewer specialist, self-evaluation, or configurable?
- What format does the evaluator return? (scores per aspect, pass/fail, detailed feedback)
- How does the orchestrator parse evaluator output? (structured JSON vs natural language)
- How does feedback flow from evaluator → orchestrator → next delegation?
- What if evaluator is inconsistent between iterations?
- Should evaluator be the same specialist every time or configurable per loop?

Reference: Claude Code /goal uses a separate evaluator model (Haiku). pi-ralph-loop uses completion markers. User wants scored rubrics with per-aspect feedback.

Blocked by: none (frontier ticket)
```

**Step 4: Create new ticket "Loop Setup UX"**

```bash
gh issue create --repo shivam2014/pi-files \
  --title "Loop Setup UX" \
  --label "wayfinder:research" \
  --body 'Parent: #105

## Question

How does the orchestrator gather all information needed to run a loop?

The user wants to say "resolve all GitHub issues" and the orchestrator must collect:
- Goal: what to achieve
- Success criteria: measurable conditions (scoring rubric, acceptance criteria)
- Who does the work: which specialist(s) implement
- Who evaluates: which specialist scores results
- Hard stops: max iterations, max tokens

Decide:
- Is this interactive (orchestrator asks questions) or structured (user provides all upfront)?
- What command/tool triggers loop mode? New /loop command? Extension to plan()?
- What if user gives vague goal? How does orchestrator probe for criteria?
- Can loop setup be saved/reused? (e.g., "loop config" file)
- How does loop interact with existing plan() system? Separate? Wrap?

Reference: Claude Code /goal takes a string "all tests pass and lint is clean". pi-ralph-loop uses RALPH.md with YAML frontmatter. User wants the orchestrator to be thorough in gathering criteria before starting.

Blocked by: none (frontier ticket)
```

**Step 5: Create new ticket "Feedback Loop Mechanics"**

```bash
gh issue create --repo shivam2014/pi-files \
  --title "Feedback Loop Mechanics" \
  --label "wayfinder:research" \
  --body 'Parent: #105

## Question

How does feedback flow through the orchestrator loop?

Example flow:
1. Orchestrator delegates to coder: "implement feature X"
2. Orchestrator delegates to reviewer: "score implementation against rubric"
3. Reviewer returns: [9, 6, 9] — field 2 failed ("logic unclear")
4. Orchestrator re-delegates to coder: "fix field 2, here is reviewer feedback: [exact feedback]"
5. Coder fixes, returns result
6. Orchestrator delegates to reviewer again: re-score
7. Score: [9, 9, 9] → done

Decide:
- What format is the feedback? (structured JSON scores + text, or natural language?)
- Does the orchestrator summarize/clean feedback before passing to coder?
- Can the coder see previous iterations results?
- Does the orchestrator include what WAS correct in the feedback? ("fields 1 and 3 are good, focus on field 2")
- What happens if the coder fixes one thing but breaks another?
- Should there be a "rolling summary" of what changed across iterations?

Blocked by: Evaluation Mechanism (needs to know evaluator output format)
```

Return confirmation of all operations and the new issue numbers/URLs.
